L. 111–3section 1397ee of this title“(1)authorize the waiver of any provision of title XIX or XXI of the Social Security Act ( et seq., 1397aa et seq.) that is not otherwise authorized to be waived under such titles or under title XI of such Act ( et seq.) as of the date of enactment of this Act [];42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1301Feb. 8, 2006“(2)imply congressional approval of any waiver, experimental, pilot, or demonstration project affecting funds made available under the State children’s health insurance program under title XXI of the Social Security Act ( et. seq.) or any amendment to such a waiver or project that has been approved as of such date of enactment; or42 U.S.C. 1397aa“(3)apply to any waiver, experimental, pilot, or demonstration project that would allow funds made available under title XXI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) to be used to provide child health assistance or other health benefits coverage to a nonpregnant childless adult that is approved before the date of enactment of this Act or to any extension, renewal, or amendment of such a waiver or project that is approved on or after such date of enactment.”42 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.heading: Strategic objectives and performance goals
children.label: a Strategic objectives and performance goals
A
children.text: the strategic objectives,

children.label: B
children.text: the performance goals, and

children.label: C
children.text: the performance measures,

children.heading: Strategic objectives
children.label: 2 Strategic objectives
children.text: Such plan shall identify specific strategic objectives relating to increasing the extent of creditable health coverage among targeted low-income children and other low-income children.

children.heading: Performance goals
children.label: 3 Performance goals
children.text: Such plan shall specify one or more performance goals for each such strategic objective so identified.

children.label: A
children.text: measured through objective, independently verifiable means, and

children.label: B
children.text: compared against performance goals, in order to determine the State’s performance under this subchapter.

children.heading: Records, reports, audits, and evaluation
children.label: b Records, reports, audits, and evaluation

children.heading: Data collection, records, and reports
children.label: 1 Data collection, records, and reports
children.text: A State child health plan shall include an assurance that the State will collect the data, maintain the records, and furnish the reports to the Secretary, at the times and in the standardized format the Secretary may require in order to enable the Secretary to monitor State program administration and compliance and to evaluate and compare the effectiveness of State plans under this subchapter.

children.heading: State assessment and study
children.label: 2 State assessment and study
children.text: A State child health plan shall include a description of the State’s plan for the annual assessments and reports under and the evaluation required by .section 1397hh(a) of this titlesection 1397hh(b) of this title

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 3 Audits
children.text: A State child health plan shall include an assurance that the State will afford the Secretary access to any records or information relating to the plan for the purposes of review or audit.

children.heading: Program development process
children.label: c Program development process
children.text: A State child health plan shall include a description of the process used to involve the public in the design and implementation of the plan and the method for ensuring ongoing public involvement.

children.heading: Program budget
children.label: d Program budget
children.text: A State child health plan shall include a description of the budget for the plan. The description shall be updated periodically as necessary and shall include details on the planned use of funds and the sources of the non-Federal share of plan expenditures, including any requirements for cost-sharing by beneficiaries.

children.heading: Application of certain general provisions
children.label: e Application of certain general provisions
A
children.text: (relating to conflict of interest standards).Section 1396a(a)(4)(C) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: (relating to third party liability).Section 1396a(a)(25) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: (relating to termination of participation of certain providers).Section 1396a(a)(39) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: (relating to enrollment of providers participating in State plans providing medical assistance on a fee-for-service basis).Section 1396a(a)(78) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: (relating to limiting FQHC contracting for provision of dental services).Section 1396a(a)(72) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: (relating to requiring certain States to seek advice from designees of Indian Health Programs and Urban Indian Organizations).Section 1396a(a)(73) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: Subsections (a)(77) and (kk) of (relating to provider and supplier screening, oversight, and reporting requirements) and subsection (a)(83) of (relating to searchable directories of the providers described in subsection (mm) of such section).section 1396a of this titlesection 1396a of this title

children.label: H
children.text: (relating to address information for enrollees and prevention of simultaneous enrollments).Section 1396a(a)(88) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: (relating to the State option to rely on findings from an Express Lane agency to help evaluate a child’s eligibility for medical assistance).Section 1396a(e)(13) of this title

children.label: J
children.text: (relating to income determined using modified adjusted gross income and household income).Section 1396a(e)(14) of this title

children.label: K
children.text: Paragraphs (5) and (16) of (relating to the State option to provide medical assistance consisting of full benefits during pregnancy and throughout the 12-month postpartum period under subchapter XIX), if the State provides child health assistance for targeted low-income children who are pregnant or to targeted low-income pregnant women and the State has elected to apply such paragraph (16) with respect to pregnant women under subchapter XIX, the provision of assistance under the State child health plan or waiver for targeted low-income children or targeted low-income pregnant women during pregnancy and the 12-month postpartum period shall be required and not at the option of the State and shall include coverage of all items or services provided to a targeted low-income child or targeted low-income pregnant woman (as applicable) under the State child health plan or waiver).section 1396a(e) of this title11So in original. There are two closing parentheses for the opening parenthesis preceding “relating”.1

children.label: L
children.text: (relating to 1 year of continuous eligibility for children), except that a targeted low-income child enrolled under the State child health plan or waiver may be transferred to the Medicaid program under subchapter XIX for the remaining duration of the 12-month continuous eligibility period, if the child becomes eligible for full benefits under subchapter XIX during such period.Section 1396a(e)(12) of this title

children.label: M
children.text: (relating to payment for services provided by Federally-qualified health centers and rural health clinics).Section 1396a(bb) of this title

children.label: N

children.label: O

children.label: P
children.text: (relating to limitation on payment with respect to managed care).Section 1396b(m)(3) of this title

children.label: Q
children.text: Paragraph (4) of (relating to optional coverage of categories of lawfully residing immigrant children or pregnant women), but only if the State has elected to apply such paragraph with respect to such category of children or pregnant women under subchapter XIX.section 1396b(v) of this title

children.label: R
children.text: (relating to payments for medical assistance furnished to aliens), except in relation to payments for services provided under .Section 1396b(v)(5) of this titlesection 1397ee(a)(1)(D)(ii) of this title

children.label: T

children.label: U
children.text: Subsections (a)(2)(C) (relating to Indian enrollment), (d)(5) (relating to contract requirement for managed care entities), (d)(6) (relating to enrollment of providers participating with a managed care entity), and (h) (relating to special rules with respect to Indian enrollees, Indian health care providers, and Indian managed care entities) of .section 1396u–2 of this title

children.label: V
children.text: (relating to authorization to receive data directly relevant to eligibility determinations).Section 1396w–2 of this title

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: (relating to administrative and judicial review), but only insofar as consistent with this subchapter.Section 1316 of this title

children.label: E

The Secretary may not approve a waiver, experimental, pilot, or demonstration project that would allow funds made available under this subchapter to be used to provide child health assistance or other health benefits coverage to a nonpregnant childless adult or a parent (as defined in ), who is not pregnant, of a targeted low-income child.section 1397kk(c)(2)(A) of this title

The Secretary may not approve, extend, renew, or amend a waiver, experimental, pilot, or demonstration project with respect to a State after , that would waive or modify the requirements of .February 4, 2009section 1397kk of this title

children.heading: Use of blended risk pools
children.label: g Use of blended risk pools

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter (or any other provision of Federal law) shall be construed as preventing a State from considering children enrolled in a qualified CHIP look-alike program and children enrolled in a State child health plan under this subchapter (or a waiver of such plan) as members of a single risk pool.

children.label: A
children.text: under which children who are under the age of 19 and are not eligible to receive medical assistance under subchapter XIX or child health assistance under this subchapter may purchase coverage through the State that provides benefits that are at least identical to the benefits provided under the State child health plan under this subchapter (or a waiver of such plan); and

children.label: B
children.text: that is funded exclusively through non-Federal funds, including funds received by the State in the form of premiums for the purchase of such coverage.
